title: Multi-modal 3D image reconstruction using M²aia software
author: Jonas Cordes
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bvq8n5zw

steps.instruction: Open the Preferences of M²aia, e.g. from the menu: Window > Preferences or Ctrl+P. Select M²aia's preference page and set the paths to the elastix and transformix executables, respectively.
Open the Data view, e.g. from the menu: Window > Show View > Data Open the Imaging tab in the Data view. enable TIC normalization disable smoothing disable baseline correction
Load the dataset into M²aia: File > Open File or Ctrl + O. Open all lipid files at once: Ensure, that all *.nrrd and *.ibd files are available at the same directory level.
Load the dataset into M²aia: File > Open File or Ctrl + O. Open all peptide files at once: Ensure, that all *.nrrd and *.ibd files are available at the same directory level.

Setup Standard Display for 2D visualization (optional). When the export utility saves the original world coordinate origin (with respect to the MSI acquisition device), the image origin is available in world coordinates. If several such datasets were loaded at the same time, they may appear stacked or may be offset. Open the Options tab in the Data view. Set Rows to 4 in the Tile images box and hit Apply . Maximize the top view display by pressing the blue square at the top right of the display menu.
Open the Data view, e.g. from the menu: Window > Show View > Data or click on the Data view tab. Navigate to the Ion imaging box and create ion images at m/z 2250 +/- 50 Da. and m/z 865 +/- 50 ppm.

Pre-align images (optional). This can increase the registration accuracy. Open the Position view: Window > Show View > Position. Open the Data Manager view: Window > Show View > Data Manager. Select image nodes in the Data Manager and use the Position view to align the images.

Open the Reconstruction 3D view, e.g. from the menu: Window > Show View > Reconstruction 3D Select the Image selection tab. Drag&Drop all peptide images to the right table. Select a reference slice in the left table (with lipid images) and click "Start stacking". Progress can be traced in the console window. Two new nodes are listed in Data Manager (Stack_0 -> lipids, Stack_1 -> peptides).
Minimize the top view again to see all render windows by pressing the blue square at the top right of the display menu.
Select one or both nodes of the image stacks in the Data Manager and perform a Reinit and " Show only selected nodes " by right-clicking on the node(s).
In the Data view, select the Initialization tab and enable Only visible. Now perform 3D ion imaging by using the Spectrum view or the Ion imaging utilities (see step 6.).
